title: Xbox layoffs affect id Software and The Elder Scrolls Online, with doubts about the future of id Tech
summary: Restructurings at Microsoft Gaming impact key studios, as executives and employees react to the cuts.

title: Marvel's Wolverine shows a spectacular trailer with Lady Deathstrike and Sabretooth
summary: Sony releases a new preview of Insomniac's game, which features several villains and an as-yet unconfirmed release date.

title: Marathon will have an experimental roguelite-style PvE mode, available for only two weeks in July
summary: Bungie details the experimental mode of its upcoming shooter, which will launch for a limited time before its full release in season 3.

title: Castlevania: Belmont's Curse impresses in previews as a return to classic action of the series
summary: Early reviews highlight its fast gameplay, its homage to the Netflix anime, and its potential to become a modern classic.

title: God of War series changes actor for Kratos after serious injury to Ryan Hurst
summary: Amazon Prime Video decides to look for a new actor for the lead role after the accident during an action scene.

title: EA insists on in-game advertising as a great business opportunity
summary: Company executives encourage developers to integrate ads from the design phase to maximize revenue.

title: Hasbro and Nintendo partner to launch The Legend of Zelda toys
summary: The collaboration includes figures and sets inspired by the saga, with the first products to be revealed soon.

title: Mortal Shell 2 physical editions sell out quickly and players ask for more stock
summary: High demand for the soulslike causes pre-orders to sell out in several stores, generating criticism of the distribution strategy.

title: Grand Theft Auto 6: news on the hacker's trial, console priority, and sales forecasts
summary: Rockstar maintains its strategy of releasing GTA VI first on consoles, while a sentence for the infiltrator is expected and record numbers are forecast.

title: Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 shows gameplay new features and a new Kill Block mode
summary: Infinity Ward presents improved visuals, a Halo-inspired vehicle, and controversy over keyboard and mouse compatibility.

title: GameStop CEO calls physical games 'completely irrelevant'
summary: The executive states that software accounts for less than 12% of the business and that the company focuses on other products.

title: Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic remake could launch in 2028
summary: Saber Interactive is confident of having the game ready by that date, although they acknowledge it's an ambitious goal.

title: Hot Wheels Infinite Rush comes to Switch 2 in September with open world
summary: The racing game moves its release date up to September 10, including a free exploration mode.

title: Matt Damon, from turning down a Bourne game to appearing in Fortnite
summary: The actor reveals that he turned down a multi-million dollar offer for a video game if it wasn't more like Myst, and now he's a playable character in Fortnite.

title: Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced receives a New Game+ mode and bug fixes
summary: The update fixes unexpected mutinies and improves the weather, while the development of the anticipated additional mode is confirmed.

title: A user creates physical cartridges for their Steam library with SSDs
summary: An ingenious system allows Steam games to be inserted into casings similar to console cartridges, functioning as an external drive.

title: Players vent their anger at PlayStation over abandonment of physical format
summary: Social media and PlayStation's YouTube channel are flooded with negative comments after announcements about the end of discs.

title: Switch 2 surpasses 6 million units sold in Japan
summary: Famitsu figures confirm the continued success of Nintendo's console in the Japanese market.

title: Christopher Nolan's Odyssey crushes box office and critics with 96% on Rotten Tomatoes
summary: The epic film is hailed as a visual masterpiece, while the director of Halo joins the Odysseus spin-off series.

title: Marvel fires its most controversial editor and moves headquarters to Los Angeles
summary: The publisher breaks with a tradition of nearly 100 years and moves to the West Coast as part of a radical restructuring.
